header.stance: 實質利率維持限制性，前端壓力緩解而長端再度趨陡

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開篇論點

6月8日美國公債收盤延續了週五就業數據衝擊所確立的高實質利率環境，但改變了殖利率曲線的表現形式。2年期殖利率下跌2個基點，10年期上升1個基點，30年期增加2個基點，使週五強烈的熊市趨平轉為小幅熊市趨陡。這一逆轉並未恢復寬鬆敘事，而是顯示，就業數據重新定價向前端延伸的最初走勢，遭遇足以令其消退的家庭與勞動市場預期轉弱資訊；與此同時，投資人仍持續要求殖利率曲線遠端提供更高的實質補償。

盤初呈現較為明確的緊縮敘事。美國就業成長強勁，仍是市場預期聯準會下一次方向性行動可能升息的主要依據；以色列與伊朗再度互相攻擊，也在亞洲交易時段推高原油價格。然而到了紐約收盤，前端已經回穩。紐約聯準銀行的5月家庭調查顯示，1年期通膨預期小幅下降，同時找到工作的前景惡化、主觀失業風險上升，且對無法按時償債的擔憂增加。這些數據並未推翻就業成長強勁的事實，但對成長與家庭信貸管道正在全面加速的看法構成挑戰。

通膨拆解使訊號更加清晰。中期與長期實質殖利率上升，10年期損益兩平通膨率則下滑。因此，油價仍是政策上的複雜因素，而非當日名目殖利率上升的直接來源。市場仍對存續期間設定高門檻，因為具韌性的需求、全球政策競爭、資本形成與主權債供給都需要實質資產負債表承接能力；但在6月8日，市場停止增加長期通膨補償。

全球市場進一步印證這種分化。韓國由AI帶動的擴張、貨幣疲弱與財政不確定性，已引發當地債券顯著拋售；歐洲政策制定者則正在權衡能源推動的通膨與活動惡化。德國工廠訂單發出明確的成長警訊，但歐元區通膨與通膨預期仍使ECB的公信力問題揮之不去。這些力量推高全球存續期間的出清利率，卻未對央行還能緊縮多少形成一致結論。

信用與跨資產表現使市場走勢維持有序。高收益率基金小幅上漲、半導體類股強勁反彈、波動率下降，但投資級與新興市場債券基金則走低。短期國庫券標售順利出清，並未出現融資斷裂；規劃中的私募與準主權融資則顯示，資本需求依然龐大且具選擇性。整體判斷是，這是在仍具限制性的環境中，由實質殖利率主導的趨陡走勢：週五的政策衝擊持續存在，家庭預期轉弱緩和了其在前端的表現，而全球存續期間競爭則在長端承擔更多壓力。

## 美國公債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債殖利率曲線依到期期限明顯分化。2年期殖利率收於4.15%，下跌2個基點；5年期持平於4.29%。10年期上升1個基點至4.56%，30年期則上升2個基點至5.03%。2s10s斜率擴大3個基點至41，2s30s擴大4個基點至88。以絕對幅度而言，單日變動不大，但其方向顯示前端收復失地，而長端仍持續反映嚴峻的實質利率與存續期間環境。

拉長比較期間，可更準確看待單日趨陡走勢。過去1週，2年期與5年期殖利率仍分別上升10與11個基點，相較之下，10年期上升9個基點，30年期上升4個基點。過去1個月則分別上升25、27、18與8個基點。累積漲幅仍集中於前端與中段，因這些區段最受政策時點影響。6月8日只是調整了這波重新定價的形狀，並未將其消除。

曲率也顯示這是1場受控的重新分配。2s5s10s蝶式利差上升1個基點至-13，使5年期區段相對兩翼仍略顯便宜。2s10s30s蝶式利差上升2個基點至-6。兩者皆不表示存在孤立的市場失序。中段仍承擔政策利率路徑可能上移的壓力；長債的相對落後幅度，則恰好足以在週五衝擊集中於前端後，引入更多期限與供給補償。

亞洲交易時段最初延續就業數據公布後的反應。2年期與5年期殖利率的升幅超過較長天期，交換利率再次反映截至12月升息25個基點，以及約16%的第2次升息機率。強勁就業數據已消除大部分預防性政策行動的理由，中東再起的攻擊則增加能源限制。紐約收盤顯示，這波最初動能並非最後結果：即使限制性政策的整體預期維持不變，2年期殖利率仍扭轉了亞洲時段的弱勢。

紐約聯準銀行調查提供了主要的國內限定條件。1年期通膨預期中位數由3.6%降至3.5%，3年期與5年期預期則維持在3.1%與3.0%。同時，受訪者主觀認為失業的機率升至15.1%，失業後找到另一份工作的機率則降至43.7%。家庭認為無法支付最低債務還款額的機率也升至12.6%，增加1.2個百分點，同時預期支出成長放緩。

這些調查細節提供了理由，使政策敏感區段得以抵抗另一次單日拋售。近期通膨預期下降，降低了立即外推能源衝擊的必要性；勞動市場信心轉弱，則令人質疑強勁的就業成長還能與家庭謹慎並存多久。預期還款壓力上升本身並不是寬鬆訊號，因為循環信用增加可能同時反映需求與壓力。但這確實使週五「需求具韌性」的結論不再如此單一，也有助於解釋2年期殖利率為何收低。

實質殖利率走勢使美國公債的變動未演變為鴿派逆轉。5年期TIPS殖利率上升1個基點至1.82%，7年期上升2個基點至2.01%，10年期上升2個基點至2.21%。20年期與30年期實質殖利率分別上升2與3個基點，至2.57%與2.77%。漲幅朝長端擴大的走勢，符合市場因存續期間、全球資本競爭及名目成長持續性的不確定性而要求更高實質補償的情境。

儘管WTI與Brent均上漲，10年期損益兩平通膨率仍下跌1個基點至2.35%。這種組合延續了週五已可見的模式：名目殖利率曲線並非在回應長期通膨預期單日再次脫錨。高昂能源成本仍會壓縮Fed因應活動轉弱的空間，並提高第2輪效應的風險。然而在6月8日，市場將邊際調整放在實質殖利率，而非通膨補償。

現金國庫券同樣呈現細緻分化。4週期票息等值殖利率持平於3.68%，6週期下跌1個基點至3.67%，8週期維持在3.69%。13週期殖利率上升3個基點至3.74%，17週期上升1個基點至3.76%，26週期上升2個基點至3.82%。52週期殖利率則下跌3個基點至3.82%。最短天期保持穩定，顯示並無迫切的流動性壓力，但中期國庫券仍對政策與供給敏感。

已完成的標售呈現不一但有序的需求訊號。美國財政部接受了$890.01億的91天期國庫券投標，最高得標利率為3.640%，投標倍數2.79，高於先前的2.69。間接投標者承接56.22%，直接投標者承接5.87%，交易商承接37.90%。$770億的182天期國庫券標售以3.690%出清，投標倍數為2.75，低於先前的2.84，交易商占比為40.56%。由於未保留發行前交易參考值，相關資訊應著重於已提供的投標倍數與配售結構，而非標售尾差或穿透幅度。

因此，這些標售並未得出一致結論。13週期需求改善，包括間接投標者參與度上升；26週期的投標倍數則較弱，且交易商占比高於較短期國庫券。兩者均於6月11日交割，因此其已完成配售代表明確的現金事件，而非對附息券需求的判斷。配合極短期國庫券殖利率穩定，結果支持前端融資維持有序，但投資人仍依期限加以區分。

6月8日最終緩和的是緊縮重新定價的速度，而非其實質內容。週五強勁的勞動市場數據使實質政策預期維持高檔，家庭調查則恢復了1股可信的下行抗衡力量，損益兩平通膨率下降也阻止油價上漲主導收盤走勢。小幅趨陡因而是合乎邏輯的結果：前端殖利率可以回吐1部分即時衝擊，但中期與長期實質殖利率仍須在全球政策不確定性、資本支出與龐大存續期間承接需求之間完成出清。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

信用市場總報酬分化，而非全面轉為防禦。LQD下跌0.1017%，符合長期實質殖利率上升時，其較高美國公債存續期間曝險的表現。HYG上漲0.1385%，JNK上漲0.0731%，與股市風險偏好改善及波動率下降一致。不同走向並不能證明現券利差的變動，但顯示6月8日並非全面撤出企業風險。利率存續期間與較低品質信用風險正透過不同管道定價。

在這種分化之下，前端政府融資仍運作正常。2次國庫券標售合計承接$1660.01億的核准供給，其中13週期投標倍數較強，26週期則較弱。較長天期標售中的交易商參與度較高，但2次結果均未伴隨最短期國庫券出現壓力。這使信用投資人能將重點放在發行人架構與資本需求，而非眼前的現金短缺。

企業融資案源凸顯交易包裝的重要性，勝過單純的資產負債表風險。Blackstone正在研究1筆超過$20億的交易，擬將私募投資基金持股打包為債券，可能成為近期同類型發行中規模較大者之一。Arcmont與Ares也正主導1筆€11億貸款，提供給Silver Lake持有的軟體公司Cegid。這2項發展均顯示機構資金仍有承接能力，但僅有委任安排，最終讓利、訂單簿品質與次級市場表現仍未確定。

科技投資仍是未來融資競爭的主要來源。Moonshot AI正尋求最多$20億的股權融資，估值為$300億；半導體類股反彈則顯示，投資人在週五拋售後並未放棄成長主題。股權融資可以降低近期企業槓桿，但相關擴張仍需要資料中心、電力、晶片及配套基礎設施。即使風險偏好改善，這種資本密集度仍使私部門發行與主權債存續期間相互競爭。

較晚出現的發展擴大了債券案源，但不屬於已完成的收盤。紐約時間下午5點過後不久，市場傳出Tencent正準備發行約$30億的雙幣債券，最早可能於週二定價。這項計畫將1家大型亞洲科技借款人納入原已繁忙的資本市場日程。其幣別配置、最終期限組合、定價調整、訂單簿與配售結果，將決定這筆交易代表廣泛需求，或是依賴集中的買方基礎。

因此，初級市場仍然開放，但承接具選擇性。已完成的國庫券供給獲得有序需求，高收益率基金持穩，規模可觀的私募交易也持續籌組。同時，多項企業融資尚無最終條款，因此不能將委任公告視為已完成承接。證券設計、贊助方與投資人分群仍居核心地位；長端實質利率門檻提高，也使依賴遠期現金流或反覆再融資的架構成本上升。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

歐洲的利率辯論同時面臨通膨限制與成長基礎轉弱。歐元區消費者通膨已達3.2%，ECB政策光譜上的官員愈來愈不願忽略能源成本，除非能確信通膨預期仍然錨定。企業計畫調高價格，以及家庭通膨預期偏高，強化了維護公信力的論點。然而，政策理由並非單向，因為活動調查與各國數據均顯示，需求環境遠比美國脆弱。

德國製造業訂單具體呈現這種脆弱性。4月訂單較3月下降3.8%，相較公開行事曆預期下降1.2%；前月數據則修正為成長4.5%。國內需求下滑2.9%，海外訂單下降4.2%，歐元區訂單更大跌11.1%。訂單仍較1年前高出1.6%，但2月至4月期間較前3個月減少3.1%，顯示工業動能交接不佳。

對德國公債而言，訂單逆轉透過成長管道支撐存續期間，並警示市場不應將因應能源的政策行動視為長期緊縮週期的開端。對美國公債而言，其傳導機制則透過相對利率與全球投資人配置運作。即使生產疲弱最終限制升息週期，決心維護公信力的ECB仍可將歐洲前端殖利率維持在足以與美元資產競爭的水準。由於通膨與成長訊號方向相反，這種張力會提高跨境波動。

南韓更鮮明地呈現成長、通膨與供給如何共同對債券不利。當地政府債券年內以本幣計價已下跌7.5%，基準3年期殖利率升至約3.9%，為2023年以來最高。AI與半導體投資使央行將成長預測由2.0%上調至2.6%，核心通膨則達2.5%。韓元疲弱與進口能源成本上升，也對原已受到晶片週期支撐的國內經濟增添壓力。

韓國交換利率至少反映3次升息，將使政策利率由2.5%升至3.25%。財政不確定性加劇了這波走勢，因另一次追加預算可能需要增加政府借款。當局試圖緩和波動，將6月計畫債券銷售量較5月削減約21%，且減量集中於較長天期。這項干預可能減緩調整速度，但無法消除名目成長增強、匯率轉嫁與未來供給可能增加的根本組合。

韓國的情況影響不僅限於當地殖利率曲線。半導體榮景可以強化區域需求與企業獲利，但同時也增加電力、建設與融資需求。當地殖利率上升後，持有外國債券所需的報酬門檻隨之提高；匯率疲弱也會使未避險美元曝險的波動加大。即使科技週期支撐全球風險資產，這仍構成美國公債存續期間的另一項競爭來源。

日本機構投資人的行為提供了重要抗衡力量。常被視為退休基金代理指標的銀行信託帳戶，5月淨買入¥3.16兆的海外債券，創現有數列紀錄。美國10年期殖利率當月曾達4.69%，以兩者各自高點計算，較相近期限的日本殖利率高約200個基點。此買盤顯示，較高的美國公債殖利率可吸引結構性需求；不過JGB殖利率上升，以及Fed與中東局勢的不確定性，仍使進場時點相當敏感。

因此，全球存續期間受到壓力，但並未遭到放棄。歐洲成長疲弱與日本退休基金買盤可以穩定需求；ECB公信力疑慮、韓國由AI帶動的景氣循環、亞洲貨幣疲弱與財政不確定性，則推高供給完成出清所需的利率。6月8日美國長期實質殖利率上升，符合買方基礎受到爭奪的環境。這不需要各央行採取單一同步路徑，而是反映多條主權債殖利率曲線在各自不同的通膨、成長與發債風險組合下競逐投資人。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場債券面臨好壞參半的外部環境。隨美國長期實質殖利率上升，EMB下跌0.1258%，儘管美元指數下滑0.02%，美國高收益率基金則上漲。WTI與Brent走高，再度加重燃料進口國的貿易條件負擔，但未引發美元全面大漲。這種組合有利於按國家與資本結構進行區分，而非形成單一的新興市場風險方向。

印尼處於這種分化的核心附近。當地債券與貨幣承壓，投資人質疑政府的干預性政策議程，使印尼盾跌至歷史低點。主權美元債則較為穩定：截至週五，一籃子政府債券對美國公債利差約為71個基點，年內僅擴大3個基點；亞洲高評級美元債利差則收窄13個基點至歷史低點50。當地信心與外部信用定價因而傳達不同訊號。

Danantara將這項分歧轉化為實際的準主權融資考驗。這家國家投資基金正準備發行美元債券，並已告知部分投資人，部分所得款項可能用於再融資年初取得的1筆$10億貸款。儘管該基金監管近900家國營企業，其發行備忘錄仍載明政府不會為票據提供擔保。一般企業用途、再融資以及缺乏明確主權擔保，使該項債務的法律支持低於印尼共和國的直接債權。

這項計畫也與Danantara擬發行、票息為3%的本幣Merah Putih債券並存，其票息低於現行市場利率。美元發債可能分散融資來源並延長期限，但也會使市場更關注貨幣錯配與投資人信心。最終證券架構、讓利與訂單簿將顯示，買方主要將該基金視為策略性國家工具，還是會針對契約上缺乏擔保進行定價。

菲律賓監管機關指出另一項企業美元流動性風險。大型企業集團在2027年至2029年間約有1.6兆披索、即$260億的債務到期，接近其債務總額的25%。未來5年，美元債務平均占企業集團債務的37.6%。披索疲弱且燃料幾乎完全依賴進口，正值美國公債實質殖利率上升、推高再融資基準利率之際，將加重本幣負擔。

菲律賓的這項曝險屬於企業，而非主權。金融穩定評估指出，企業已透過債券、銀行貸款與內部流動性滿足再融資需求，銀行也保有足夠資本吸收意外損失。然而，房地產估值偏高與無擔保消費信貸擴張，增添了國內脆弱性。系統性緩衝降低了立即傳染風險，但並未消除管理到期債務集中與外匯曝險的必要性。

因此，新興市場債務管理風險仍高度細分。印尼面臨信心、政策界線與準主權支持問題；菲律賓企業集團則面臨具明確時程的到期牆與幣別組成問題。6月8日美元近乎持平，限制了廣泛流動性緊縮，但美國公債實質殖利率上升與油價高昂，仍使外部約束收緊。決定性變數包括可運用的外幣現金流、契約支持、再融資期限，以及當地機構能否承接債務而不迫使主權資產負債表介入。

## 跨資產

美國股市否定了週五科技股拋售時所伴隨的嚴重避險氛圍。S&P 500上漲0.2978%，Nasdaq 100上漲1.577%，費城半導體指數大漲5.6128%。VIX下跌12.0409%至18.92。這是長存續期間盈餘曝險最高資產的強勁反彈，儘管Nasdaq的週報酬仍為負值。風險偏好改善的程度足以支撐高收益率債券，但仍不足以拉低美國公債長期實質殖利率。

油價使這波復甦更為複雜。以色列與伊朗再度互相攻擊，對停火構成挑戰，WTI上漲0.8394%至$91.30，Brent上漲1.2461%至$94.25。原油上漲通常會對損益兩平通膨率與政策敏感殖利率構成上行壓力，但10年期損益兩平通膨率下降，2年期殖利率也收低。市場將能源視為央行政策彈性尚未解決的限制，而非新一波單日通膨螺旋。

美元指數幾乎持平於100.05，下跌0.02%；黃金下滑0.0277%至$4,335.90。美元與金價持平、波動率下降、股市上漲，並不符合傳統的避險搶進情境。這更符合週五實質利率衝擊後，資金重新轉向成長曝險。風險資產展現韌性，也代表盤中金融條件並未收緊到足以獨立推動存續期間反彈的程度。

信用市場補完了這幅分化圖像。投資級債券的上市指標隨美國公債存續期間小幅走弱，高收益率指標上漲，新興市場債券指標則下跌。這種模式符合有序重新配置，而非全面去槓桿。跨資產表現因而強化殖利率曲線的判斷：前端政策疑慮在邊際上緩和，但強勁資本需求、油價走強與風險偏好復甦，仍使長端需要更高的實質出清殖利率。

## 前瞻市場意涵

6月10日08:30 ET公布的5月CPI、隨後6月11日08:30公布的PPI，以及6月16日至17日舉行的FOMC會議，將檢驗6月8日損益兩平通膨率下降與前端回穩能否經受實際通膨數據考驗。整體CPI預期月增0.5%、年增4.2%，前值分別為0.6%與3.8%；核心CPI月增率則預期由0.4%放緩至0.2%。可觀察的觸發因素是，能源成本轉嫁會維持集中於整體物價，還是擴散至核心商品、服務與生產者利潤率。核心項目漲勢廣度減弱，將確認家庭調查所顯示的降溫訊號，並支持當日2年期債券的相對優異表現。若數據全面高於預期，則會挑戰這項判斷、確認就業數據公布後政策利率路徑上移，並使2年至10年期實質殖利率再度承壓。

6月9日至11日的美國公債標售將檢驗有序的國庫券融資能否延伸至附息券存續期間。6月9日發行$580億的3年期公債，接著6月10日增額發行票息4.375%的$390億 10年期公債，6月11日增額發行票息5.000%的$220億 30年期公債；3者均於6月15日交割，總額為$1,190億。相對於當時發行前交易水準的出清殖利率、投標倍數、直接與間接投標占比、交易商承接比例及交割條件，都是可觀察的觸發因素。最終投資人需求強勁，將證實6月8日的趨陡仍屬總體經濟與實質利率調整。若投標疲弱，尤其出現在通膨數據公布後，供給與資產負債表承接能力將再次成為長端表現落後的獨立驅動因素。

6月8日ET下午5點過後不久傳出的以色列—伊朗有條件降溫、6月9日公布的德國4月工業生產，以及6月11日的ECB決策，共同構成全球存續期間考驗。德國產出預期在前值修正為下降0.1%後成長0.5%；公開基準預期ECB將存款利率上調25個基點至2.25%。觸發因素包括停止攻擊能否持續、原油是否回吐風險溢價、德國經濟活動是否確認訂單萎縮，以及ECB指引會將任何升息界定為預防措施還是一連串行動。局勢持久降溫且產出疲弱，將挑戰同步緊縮的看法，並緩解德國公債、美國公債損益兩平通膨率與石油進口型新興市場貨幣的壓力。攻擊再起或鷹派立場延續，則將確認全球對存續期間的競爭，並再次引發輸入性通膨壓力。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為準。

overall_summary.headline: 前端殖利率回落，但實質利率壓力仍使長端承壓
overall_summary.session_narrative: 2年期殖利率回落、長端上升，使殖利率曲線在週五就業數據衝擊後呈現小幅熊市趨陡。家庭對勞動市場的信心與近期通膨預期轉弱，抑制了前端動能，但全球政策競爭、資本需求與主權債供給推高實質存續期間補償。損益兩平通膨率下滑、國庫券市場有序且風險資產走強，限制了這波變動所釋出的壓力訊號。
overall_summary.summary: 美國公債殖利率曲線小幅熊市趨陡，因家庭對勞動市場與信貸的預期轉弱，緩和了就業數據引發的前端重新定價。長期實質殖利率仍然上升，但損益兩平通膨率下滑，加上風險資產走勢有序，使這波變動未演變為通膨或融資衝擊。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤走勢緩和了眼前的政策疑慮，但並未降低投資存續期間所面臨的實質門檻。
section_summaries.next_check: 核心通膨的廣度將顯示前端反彈能否延續。
section_summaries.summary: 6月8日延續限制性環境，但其表現形式由週五的熊市趨平轉為溫和熊市趨陡。家庭對勞動市場與信貸的預期轉弱，緩解了前端壓力；長期實質殖利率上升，則反映全球政策競爭、資本需求與主權債供給。

section_summaries.market_read: 寬鬆訊號僅限於前端，期限與實質利率壓力依然存在。
section_summaries.next_check: 標售需求與核心通膨將檢驗趨陡走勢能否維持有序。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 殖利率曲線重新分配了週五的衝擊，而非將其逆轉：對政策敏感的前端回穩，中期與長期實質殖利率則維持強勢。家庭近期通膨預期下降、就業信心轉弱帶來緩解，但強勁就業數據與存續期間競爭仍維持限制性定價。

section_summaries.market_read: 信用市場表現顯示資金正有序重新配置，而非全面撤出企業風險。
section_summaries.next_check: 最終讓利與訂單簿品質將顯示大型交易能否獲得廣泛需求。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用市場沿利率與風險管道分化；隨高收益率指標與股市同步持穩，投資級債因存續期間較長而落後。國庫券融資保持有序，大型私募融資持續醞釀，但條款尚未完備，加上長期實質利率門檻偏高，使初級市場承接仍具選擇性。

section_summaries.market_read: 海外殖利率曲線正推高長期美國公債存續期間完成出清所需的補償。
section_summaries.next_check: ECB指引、德國產出與韓國供給計畫仍是全球層面的關鍵觀察。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 全球存續期間面臨相互競爭的力量：德國訂單疲弱與日本機構買盤支撐需求，但ECB公信力疑慮，以及韓國由AI帶動的成長、貨幣疲弱與財政不確定性，則推高市場出清殖利率。結果是存續期間承接能力受到多方爭奪，而非形成同步緊縮路徑。

section_summaries.market_read: 新興市場壓力集中於個別發行人，但實質利率與能源環境縮小了其韌性空間。
section_summaries.next_check: Danantara的架構與菲律賓再融資條款將揭示美元流動性壓力。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 新興市場債券仍高度分化；美國實質殖利率上升與油價走強收緊外部約束，但美元並未全面大漲。印尼的準主權支持界線，以及菲律賓企業到期債務集中，使契約保障、外幣現金流與再融資期限成為決定性因素。

section_summaries.market_read: 風險偏好回升，但並未帶來足以推動存續期間反彈的金融緊縮。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價風險溢價與半導體漲勢的持久性，是接下來的跨資產觀察重點。
section_summaries.summary: 股市、半導體與高收益率指標反彈，波動率下降，否定了週五拋售後出現全面避險走勢的可能。油價走強限制央行的政策彈性，但損益兩平通膨率下滑且美元持平，顯示並未出現新一波通膨螺旋，長期實質殖利率因而仍是主要壓力來源。

section_summaries.market_read: 只有在通膨、標售需求與全球政策未同時對存續期間不利的情況下，現行環境才能延續。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注實際核心通膨、附息券投標者品質，以及全球局勢能否持續降溫。
section_summaries.summary: 3項相互關聯的考驗，將決定收盤時前端壓力緩解、長端承壓的局面能否延續：CPI、PPI與FOMC前後的通膨廣度；各期美國公債附息券的需求；以及中東降溫、德國生產與ECB指引所傳達的全球存續期間訊號。

watch_events.market_implication: 若漲勢擴大，將挑戰前端壓力緩解的局面，並對2年至10年期實質殖利率構成壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 核心商品、服務與生產者利潤率的漲勢擴大，超出整體能源壓力的範圍。
watch_events.title: 美國通膨與政策考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 觀察能源成本轉嫁是否仍集中於整體物價，或擴散至核心項目。

watch_events.market_implication: 若承接不佳，供給將成為長端表現落後的獨立驅動因素。
watch_events.market_trigger: 最終投資人需求疲弱，尤其若出現在通膨數據公布後，將暴露資產負債表限制。
watch_events.title: 美國公債附息券需求
watch_events.watch_line: 比較各期附息券的投標者結構、交易商承接比例與出清水準。

watch_events.market_implication: 此結果將強化存續期間競爭，並加重新興市場的輸入性通膨壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 攻擊再起或ECB延續鷹派立場，將使能源與全球利率壓力持續。
watch_events.title: 全球存續期間與局勢降溫考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 隨德國生產數據與ECB指引釐清政策組合，觀察局勢能否持續降溫。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The June 8 Treasury close preserved the high-real-rate regime established by Friday’s employment shock, but changed its curve expression. The 2-year yield fell 2 basis points, the 10-year rose 1 and the 30-year added 2, turning Friday’s forceful bear flattening into a modest bear steepening. That reversal did not restore an easing narrative. It showed that the initial front-end extension of the payroll repricing met enough weaker household and labor-expectations information to fade, even as investors continued to demand more real compensation farther out the curve.

The session began with a cleaner tightening story. Strong US hiring remained the anchor for expectations that the Federal Reserve’s next directional move could be an increase, and renewed Israeli-Iranian strikes pushed crude higher during Asian trading. By the New York close, however, the front end had recovered. The New York Fed’s May household survey combined slightly lower 1-year inflation expectations with worse job-finding prospects, higher perceived job-loss risk and more concern about missed debt payments. Those readings did not overturn payroll strength, but they challenged the idea that the growth and household-credit channels were uniformly accelerating.

Inflation decomposition sharpened the message. Real yields rose across intermediate and long maturities while 10-year breakeven inflation slipped. Oil therefore remained a policy complication rather than the direct source of the day’s nominal-yield increase. The market was still assigning a high hurdle to duration because resilient demand, global policy competition, capital formation and sovereign supply require real balance-sheet capacity, yet it stopped adding to long-run inflation compensation on June 8.

Global markets reinforced that split. Korea’s AI-led expansion, weak currency and fiscal uncertainty have driven a pronounced local bond selloff, while European policymakers are weighing energy-driven inflation against deteriorating activity. German factory orders delivered a clear growth warning, but euro-area inflation and expectations kept the ECB’s credibility problem alive. These forces raise the global clearing rate for duration without producing a synchronized verdict on how far central banks can tighten.

Credit and cross assets kept the move orderly. High-yield funds edged higher, semiconductor shares rebounded sharply and volatility fell, even as investment-grade and emerging-market debt funds lost ground. Short-bill auctions cleared without a funding rupture, while planned private and quasi-sovereign financings showed that capital demand remains large and selective. The governing interpretation is a real-yield-led steepening inside a still-restrictive regime: Friday’s policy shock persisted, weaker household expectations softened its front-end expression, and global duration competition carried more of the burden at the long end.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

The Treasury curve separated cleanly by maturity. The 2-year yield closed at 4.15%, down 2 basis points, and the 5-year held at 4.29%. The 10-year rose 1 basis point to 4.56%, while the 30-year increased 2 to 5.03%. The 2s10s slope widened 3 basis points to 41, and 2s30s widened 4 to 88. The daily move was small in outright terms, but its direction showed the front end regaining ground as the long end continued to price a demanding real-rate and duration backdrop.

Longer comparisons keep the daily steepening in perspective. Over 1 week, 2- and 5-year yields were still higher by 10 and 11 basis points, compared with nine at 10 years and four at 30 years. Over 1 month, the increases were 25, 27, 18 and 8 basis points, respectively. The accumulated move remains concentrated in the front and belly, where policy timing carries the most weight. June 8 adjusted the shape around that repricing rather than erasing it.

Curvature also pointed to a controlled redistribution. The 2s5s10s butterfly rose 1 basis point to -13, leaving the 5-year sector modestly cheap to its wings. The 2s10s30s butterfly increased 2 basis points to -6. Neither move suggests an isolated dislocation. The belly retained the burden of a potentially higher policy path, while the long bond underperformed just enough to introduce more term and supply compensation after Friday’s front-loaded shock.

Asian trading initially extended the payroll reaction. 2- and 5-year yields rose more than longer maturities as swaps again embedded a quarter-point increase by December and roughly a 16% chance of a 2nd move. Strong hiring had removed much of the rationale for policy insurance, and renewed Middle East strikes added an energy constraint. The New York close shows that this first impulse was not the final one: the 2-year reversed from its Asian weakness even though the broader expectation of restrictive policy remained intact.

The New York Fed survey supplied the main domestic qualification. Median 1-year inflation expectations declined to 3.5% from 3.6%, while 3- and 5-year expectations stayed at 3.1% and 3.0%. At the same time, the perceived probability of losing a job rose to 15.1%, and the probability of finding another job after a loss fell to 43.7%. Households also put the chance of missing a minimum debt payment at 12.6%, up 1.2 percentage points, as expected spending growth slowed.

Those survey details offered the policy-sensitive sector a reason to resist another daily selloff. Lower near-term inflation expectations reduce the immediate need to extrapolate the energy shock, and weaker labor confidence questions how long strong payroll gains can coexist with household caution. The rise in expected payment stress is not an easing signal by itself, since revolving credit can reflect both demand and strain. It does make Friday’s resilient-demand conclusion less one-dimensional and helps explain why 2-year yields finished lower.

Real-yield behavior prevented the Treasury move from becoming a dovish reversal. The 5-year TIPS yield rose 1 basis point to 1.82%, the 7-year increased 2 to 2.01%, and the 10-year added 2 to 2.21%. Real yields at 20 and 30 years climbed 2 and 3 basis points to 2.57% and 2.77%. The progression toward the long end fits a market requiring more real compensation for duration, global capital competition and uncertainty around the persistence of nominal growth.

The 10-year breakeven fell 1 basis point to 2.35%, even though WTI and Brent both advanced. That combination continued the pattern visible on Friday: the nominal curve was not responding to a new daily unanchoring of long-run inflation expectations. Expensive energy still narrows the Fed’s room to respond to softer activity and raises the risk of second-round effects. On June 8, however, the market placed the marginal adjustment in real yields rather than inflation compensation.

Cash bills showed similarly fine differentiation. 4-week coupon-equivalent yields were unchanged at 3.68%, 6-week yields slipped 1 basis point to 3.67%, and 8-week yields held at 3.69%. The 13-week yield rose 3 basis points to 3.74%, the 17-week added 1 to 3.76%, and the 26-week rose 2 to 3.82%. The 52-week yield moved 3 basis points lower to 3.82%. Stability at the shortest maturities argued against immediate liquidity pressure, even as intermediate bills retained policy and supply sensitivity.

Completed auctions produced mixed but orderly demand signals. Treasury accepted $89.001 billion of 91-day bills at a 3.640% high rate with 2.79 bid-to-cover, up from 2.69 previously. Indirect bidders took 56.22%, direct bidders 5.87% and dealers 37.90%. The $77 billion 182-day sale cleared at 3.690% with 2.75 cover, down from 2.84, and a 40.56% dealer share. The reported cover and allocation mix describe absorption, but the absence of a when-issued yield precludes a tail or through judgment.

The auctions therefore did not deliver one uniform verdict. Demand improved at 13 weeks, including stronger indirect participation, while the 26-week sale drew softer cover and a larger dealer share than the shorter bill. Both settle on June 11, making their completed allocations a defined cash event rather than a statement about coupon demand. Together with steady very-short bill yields, the results support orderly front-end funding but leave investors discriminating across maturity.

June 8 ultimately moderated the speed, not the substance, of the tightening repricing. Friday’s labor strength kept real policy expectations elevated, the household survey restored a credible downside counterforce, and falling breakevens prevented higher oil from dominating the close. A small steepening was the logical outcome: front-end yields could retrace some of the immediate shock, whereas intermediate and long real yields still had to clear against global policy uncertainty, capital spending and a heavy demand for duration capacity.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Credit total returns were split rather than broadly defensive. LQD fell 0.1017%, consistent with its greater Treasury-duration exposure as long real yields rose. HYG gained 0.1385% and JNK added 0.0731%, matching the improvement in equity risk appetite and lower volatility. The different directions do not establish a cash-spread move, but they show that June 8 was not a uniform withdrawal from corporate risk. Rate duration and lower-quality credit risk were being priced through different channels.

Front-end government funding remained functional beneath that split. The 2 bill auctions absorbed a combined $166.001 billion of accepted supply, with stronger cover at 13 weeks and softer cover at 26 weeks. Dealer participation was higher in the longer sale, but neither result coincided with stress at the shortest bill maturities. This allowed credit investors to focus on issuer structure and capital demand rather than on an immediate scarcity of cash.

The corporate financing pipeline emphasized packaging over plain balance-sheet risk. Blackstone was exploring a transaction of more than $2 billion that would bundle stakes in private investment funds into bonds, potentially creating one of the larger recent offerings of its kind. Arcmont and Ares were also leading a €1.1 billion loan for Silver Lake-backed software company Cegid. Both developments point to available institutional capacity, yet mandates alone leave final concession, orderbook quality and secondary performance unresolved.

Technology investment remained a major source of prospective funding competition. Moonshot AI was seeking as much as $2 billion of equity at a $30 billion valuation, and the rebound in semiconductor shares showed that investors had not abandoned the growth theme after Friday’s selloff. Equity funding can reduce near-term corporate leverage, but the underlying expansion still requires data centers, power, chips and related infrastructure. That capital intensity keeps private issuance in competition with sovereign duration even when risk appetite improves.

A later development extended the bond pipeline without belonging to the completed close. Shortly after 5 p.m. New York time, Tencent was reported to be preparing roughly $3 billion of dual-currency bonds that could price as early as Tuesday. The planned sale adds a large Asian technology borrower to an already busy capital calendar. Its currency split, final maturity mix, pricing revision, orderbook and allocation will determine whether the transaction demonstrates broad demand or relies on a concentrated buyer base.

Primary-market conditions were therefore open but selective. Completed bill supply met orderly demand, high-yield funds held up and sizable private transactions continued to be assembled. At the same time, the lack of final terms on several corporate financings prevents mandate announcements from being treated as completed absorption. Security design, sponsorship and investor segmentation remain central, and the higher long-end real-rate hurdle raises the cost of structures that depend on distant cash flows or repeated refinancing.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Europe’s rates debate combined an inflation constraint with a weakening growth base. Euro-area consumer inflation had reached 3.2%, and officials across the ECB’s policy spectrum were increasingly unwilling to look through energy costs without assurance that expectations would remain anchored. Firms’ intended price increases and elevated household inflation views strengthened the credibility argument. Yet the policy case was not one-way because activity surveys and national data pointed to a much more fragile demand environment than in the United States.

German manufacturing orders made that fragility concrete. April orders fell 3.8% from March, compared with a 1.2% decline expected in public calendars, after the prior month was revised to a 4.5% gain. Domestic demand dropped 2.9%, foreign orders declined 4.2%, and euro-area orders plunged 11.1%. Orders were still 1.6% higher than a year earlier, but the February-to-April period was 3.1% weaker than the previous 3 months, indicating a poor industrial handoff.

For Bunds, the order reversal supports duration through the growth channel and warns against treating an energy response as the start of a long tightening cycle. For Treasuries, the mechanism runs through relative rates and global investor allocation. An ECB determined to protect credibility can keep European front-end yields high enough to compete with dollar assets, even if weak production eventually caps the cycle. The tension raises cross-border volatility because the inflation and growth signals point in opposite directions.

South Korea supplied a more forceful example of growth, inflation and supply working together against bonds. Local government bonds had lost 7.5% in local-currency terms during the year, and the benchmark 3-year yield had risen to about 3.9%, its highest since 2023. AI and semiconductor investment lifted the central bank’s growth forecast to 2.6% from 2.0%, while core inflation reached 2.5%. A weak won and higher imported energy costs added pressure to a domestic economy already supported by the chip cycle.

Korean swaps were pricing at least 3 increases that would lift the policy rate to 3.25% from 2.5%. Fiscal uncertainty amplified the move because another supplementary budget could require more government borrowing. Authorities sought to temper volatility by reducing planned June bond sales about 21% from May, concentrated in longer maturities. The intervention may slow the adjustment, but it does not remove the underlying combination of stronger nominal growth, currency pass-through and possible future supply.

The Korean episode matters beyond its local curve. A semiconductor boom can strengthen regional demand and corporate earnings while simultaneously raising electricity, construction and financing needs. Higher local yields then increase the return hurdle for holding foreign bonds, and currency weakness makes unhedged dollar exposure more volatile. The result is another source of competition for Treasury duration even when the technology cycle supports global risk assets.

Japanese institutional behavior offered an important counterforce. Bank trust accounts, often used as pension-fund proxies, bought a net ¥3.16 trillion of overseas bonds in May, a record in the available series. US 10-year yields had reached 4.69% during that month, roughly 200 basis points above similar Japanese yields at their respective peaks. The buying shows that higher Treasury yields can attract structural demand, even as rising JGB yields and uncertainty over the Fed and Middle East keep the timing sensitive.

Global duration was consequently pressured, not abandoned. European growth weakness and Japanese pension buying can anchor demand, whereas ECB credibility concerns, Korea’s AI-led cycle, weak Asian currencies and fiscal uncertainty lift the rate needed to clear supply. The June 8 rise in long US real yields fits that contested buyer-base environment. It does not require a single synchronized central-bank path; it reflects multiple sovereign curves competing for investors under different combinations of inflation, growth and issuance risk.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt faced a mixed external setting. EMB fell 0.1258% as long US real yields rose, even though the dollar index slipped 0.02% and US high-yield funds gained. WTI and Brent advanced, renewing a terms-of-trade burden for fuel importers without producing a broad dollar surge. The combination favored country and capital-structure differentiation over a single EM risk direction.

Indonesia sat near the center of that differentiation. Its local bonds and currency were under pressure, and the rupiah reached a record low as investors questioned the government’s interventionist policy agenda. Sovereign dollar bonds had been more stable: a government-bond basket was about 71 basis points over Treasuries as of Friday, only 3 basis points wider for the year, while high-grade Asian dollar spreads had tightened 13 basis points to a record 50. Local confidence and external credit pricing were therefore sending different signals.

Danantara converted that divergence into a live quasi-sovereign financing test. The state investment fund was preparing a dollar bond and had told some investors that part of the proceeds could refinance a $1 billion loan obtained earlier in the year. Its offering memorandum stated that the government would not guarantee the notes, despite the fund’s oversight of nearly 900 state companies. General corporate use, refinancing and the absence of an explicit sovereign guarantee place the obligation below a direct Republic of Indonesia claim in legal support.

The planned deal also sits alongside Danantara’s proposed local-currency Merah Putih bonds carrying a 3% coupon, below prevailing market rates. Dollar issuance may diversify funding and extend maturities, but it transfers more attention to currency mismatch and investor confidence. The final security structure, concession and orderbook will indicate whether buyers treat the fund primarily as a strategic state vehicle or price the contractual absence of a guarantee.

Philippine regulators identified a separate corporate dollar-liquidity risk. Large conglomerates face about 1.6 trillion pesos, or $26 billion, of maturities from 2027 through 2029, nearly 25% of their debt. US dollar obligations average 37.6% of conglomerate debt over the next 5 years. A weak peso and near-total dependence on imported fuel increase the local-currency burden just as higher Treasury real yields raise the refinancing base rate.

That Philippine exposure belongs to corporates, not the sovereign. The financial-stability assessment said companies had met refinancing needs through bonds, bank loans and internal liquidity, and banks retained sufficient capital to absorb unexpected losses. Elevated property values and expanding unsecured consumer credit added domestic vulnerabilities, however. The systemic buffer reduces immediate contagion risk without eliminating the need to manage maturity concentration and foreign-exchange exposure.

EM debt-management risk thus remained granular. Indonesia faced confidence, policy-boundary and quasi-sovereign support questions; Philippine conglomerates faced a dated maturity wall and currency composition problem. A nearly flat dollar limited the broad liquidity squeeze on June 8, but higher real Treasury yields and expensive oil still tightened the external constraint. The decisive variables are usable currency cash flow, contractual support, refinancing tenor and whether local institutions can absorb obligations without forcing sovereign balance sheets to intervene.

## Cross asset

US equities rejected the severe risk-off tone that had accompanied Friday’s technology selloff. The S&P 500 gained 0.2978%, the Nasdaq 100 rose 1.577% and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index jumped 5.6128%. The VIX fell 12.0409% to 18.92. This was a forceful recovery in the assets most exposed to long-duration earnings, although weekly returns for the Nasdaq remained negative. Risk appetite improved enough to support high yield, but not enough to pull long Treasury real yields lower.

Oil complicated that recovery. WTI gained 0.8394% to $91.30 and Brent rose 1.2461% to $94.25 after renewed Israeli and Iranian strikes challenged the ceasefire. Higher crude normally pressures breakevens and policy-sensitive yields, yet the 10-year breakeven declined and the 2-year yield finished lower. Markets treated energy as an unresolved constraint on central-bank flexibility, not as a new daily inflation spiral.

The dollar index was almost unchanged at 100.05, down 0.02%, and gold slipped 0.0277% to $4,335.90. A flat dollar and gold price, falling volatility and rising equities do not describe a conventional haven rush. They fit a rotation back toward growth exposure after Friday’s real-rate shock. The resilience of risk assets also means that financial conditions did not tighten enough during the session to provide an independent duration rally.

Credit completed the mixed picture. Investment-grade debt’s listed gauge weakened slightly with Treasury duration, high-yield gauges rose, and the emerging-market debt gauge declined. The pattern was consistent with an orderly reallocation rather than broad deleveraging. Cross assets therefore reinforced the curve diagnosis: front-end policy fear eased at the margin, yet strong capital demand, firmer oil and recovering risk appetite left the long end requiring a higher real clearing yield.

## Forward Market Significance

May CPI on June 10 at 08:30 ET, followed by PPI on June 11 at 08:30 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, will test whether June 8’s lower breakeven and front-end recovery can survive realized inflation. Headline CPI is expected at 0.5% on the month and 4.2% on the year after 0.6% and 3.8%, with core expected to slow to 0.2% monthly from 0.4%. The observable trigger is whether energy pass-through stays concentrated in headline prices or broadens through core goods, services and producer margins. Softer core breadth would confirm the household survey’s moderating signal and support the day’s 2-year outperformance. A broad upside surprise would challenge it, confirm the post-payroll higher-policy path and place 2- to 10-year real yields under renewed pressure.

The June 9-11 Treasury sequence will test whether orderly bill funding extends into coupon duration. A $58 billion 3-year issue on June 9 is followed by a $39 billion 10-year reopening with a 4.375% coupon on June 10 and a $22 billion 30-year reopening with a 5.000% coupon on June 11; all three settle June 15 for $119 billion gross. Clearing yields against then-current when-issued levels, bid-to-cover, direct and indirect shares, dealer takedown and settlement conditions are the observable triggers. Strong end-user demand would confirm that June 8’s steepening remained a macro and real-rate adjustment. Weak bidding, especially after inflation data, would reopen supply and balance-sheet capacity as independent drivers of long-end underperformance.

The conditional Israel-Iran de-escalation reported shortly after 5 p.m. ET on June 8, Germany’s April industrial-production release on June 9 and the ECB decision on June 11 form the global duration test. German output is expected to rise 0.5% after a revised 0.1% decline, while the consensus expectation anticipates a 25-basis-point increase in the ECB deposit rate to 2.25%. The triggers are whether the halt in strikes persists, crude relinquishes its risk premium, German activity confirms the order contraction, and ECB guidance frames any increase as insurance or a sequence. Durable de-escalation and weak output would challenge synchronized tightening and relieve Bunds, Treasury breakevens and oil-importing EM currencies. Renewed attacks or hawkish follow-through would confirm global competition for duration and reopen imported-inflation pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: The close softened immediate policy fear without lowering the real hurdle for duration.
section_summaries.next_check: Core inflation breadth will show whether the front-end recovery can extend.
section_summaries.summary: June 8 preserved the restrictive regime but shifted its expression from Friday’s bear flattening to a mild bear steepening. Softer household labor and credit expectations eased pressure at the front end, while higher long real yields reflected global policy competition, capital demand and sovereign supply.

section_summaries.market_read: The easing signal is confined to the front end, with term and real-rate pressure intact.
section_summaries.next_check: Auction demand and core inflation will test whether the steepening stays orderly.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: The curve redistributed Friday’s shock rather than reversing it: the policy-sensitive front end recovered while intermediate and long real yields remained firm. Lower near-term household inflation expectations and weaker job confidence provided relief, but strong payrolls and duration competition preserved restrictive pricing.

section_summaries.market_read: Credit behavior points to orderly reallocation, not a broad retreat from corporate risk.
section_summaries.next_check: Final concessions and orderbook quality will reveal whether large deals find broad demand.
section_summaries.summary: Credit split along rate and risk channels, with investment-grade duration lagging as high-yield gauges held up alongside equities. Bill funding remained orderly and large private financings continued to form, but incomplete terms and a high long-real-rate hurdle kept primary-market absorption selective.

section_summaries.market_read: Foreign curves are raising the compensation needed to clear long Treasury duration.
section_summaries.next_check: ECB guidance, German output and Korean supply plans remain the key global checks.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Global duration faced competing forces: weak German orders and Japanese institutional buying supported demand, while ECB credibility concerns and Korea’s AI-led growth, currency weakness and fiscal uncertainty raised clearing yields. The result was contested duration capacity rather than a synchronized tightening path.

section_summaries.market_read: EM pressure is issuer-specific, but the real-rate and energy backdrop narrows resilience.
section_summaries.next_check: Danantara’s structure and Philippine refinancing terms will expose dollar-liquidity strain.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: EM debt remained highly differentiated as higher US real yields and firmer oil tightened external constraints without a broad dollar surge. Indonesia’s quasi-sovereign support boundary and Philippine corporate maturity concentration made contractual backing, currency cash flow and refinancing tenor decisive.

section_summaries.market_read: Risk appetite recovered without delivering the financial tightening needed for a duration rally.
section_summaries.next_check: Oil’s risk premium and the durability of semiconductor gains are the next cross-asset checks.
section_summaries.summary: Equities, semiconductors and high-yield gauges rebounded as volatility fell, rejecting a broad haven move after Friday’s selloff. Firmer oil constrained central-bank flexibility, yet falling breakevens and a flat dollar showed no fresh inflation spiral, leaving long real yields as the dominant pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: The regime survives only if inflation, auction demand and global policy do not align against duration.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch realized core inflation, coupon bidder quality and whether global de-escalation holds.
section_summaries.summary: 3 linked tests will determine whether the close’s front-end relief and long-end pressure persist: inflation breadth around CPI, PPI and the FOMC; demand across the Treasury coupon sequence; and global duration signals from Middle East de-escalation, German production and ECB guidance.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.21% (+2 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.35% (-1 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 41 bp (+3 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 88 bp (+4 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Front-end yields eased, but real-rate pressure kept the long end under strain
overall_summary.session_narrative: The 2-year yield retraced while the long end rose, producing a modest bear steepening after Friday’s payroll shock. Softer household labor confidence and near-term inflation expectations checked the front-end impulse, but global policy competition, capital demand and sovereign supply lifted real duration compensation. Falling breakevens, orderly bills and firmer risk assets limited the move’s stress signal.
overall_summary.summary: Treasuries modestly bear-steepened as weaker household labor and credit expectations softened the payroll-driven front-end repricing. Long real yields still rose, while lower breakevens and orderly risk assets kept the move from becoming an inflation or funding shock.

watch_events.market_implication: Broadening would challenge front-end relief and pressure 2- to 10-year real yields.
watch_events.market_trigger: Core goods, services and producer margins broaden beyond headline energy pressure.
watch_events.title: US inflation and policy test
watch_events.watch_line: Track whether energy pass-through remains in headline prices or broadens into core.

watch_events.market_implication: A weak reception would make supply an independent driver of long-end underperformance.
watch_events.market_trigger: Weak end-user demand, especially after inflation data, would expose balance-sheet limits.

watch_events.watch_line: Compare bidder mix, dealer takedown and clearing levels across the coupon sequence.

watch_events.market_implication: That outcome would reinforce duration competition and imported-inflation stress in EM.
